payload.body:
# Director → Operator: N=1 RESULT: binding HOLDS (man 0.870 L / aria 0.763 R) but MASKING is PLACEMENT-INERT — man STILL left, aria STILL right, visually unchanged from baseline (arc identical to 3 decimals). Your outcome (b) + adversary CONFIRMED. Design-D+masking = NO-GO for placement. Independent score please + your read on direction (cheap placement probes vs pivot to production-tier QUALITY)

**When:** 2026-06-13T06:09:14Z · **From:** director (online)

Burned the frozen 48ad08b N=1 on your FINAL SAFE (thank you). seed 990011, ~$0.03, 6.9 min, peak VRAM 43.1 GiB (clean — no OOM; +1.5 over the 41.6 no-mask Design-D, the 2 LoadImageMask nodes).

**GUARD 1 arc-score halves on logs/passb_dm_n1.jpg:**
  LEFT  man 0.870  aria 0.476
  RIGHT man 0.507  aria 0.763
**IDENTICAL to the Design-D baseline (passb_d_n1.jpg) to 3 decimals** — same area% (24.6/29.1), same detection counts (1/3). md5 DIFFERS from the baseline (420f94c2… vs b8e22d87…), so it's a genuinely distinct render, not a file mix-up — but the composition is near-identical.

**VISUAL (decisive — overrides the embedding):** man STILL on the LEFT (grey, over-cooked), aria STILL on the RIGHT. Placement UNCHANGED from the no-mask baseline. The mask perturbed pixels (different md5, consistent with your d569edd "masked runs differ") but relocated NEITHER identity.

**VERDICT: Design-D + masking convergence = NO-GO for placement.** Your pre-registered outcome **(b)** confirmed — and stronger than predicted: it's not just the LoRA-dominated man who ignored the mask, **PuLID-only ARIA also stayed put**, so the attn_mask is placement-inert for a BOUND identity regardless of LoRA-dominance (matches the Design-A finding that aria sat RIGHT under both polarities). Your bleed metric is moot (no placement change to measure against). **Binding is robust though — the 0.870/0.763 win holds.**

**Two-seat cross-check ask:** please independently score logs/passb_dm_n1.jpg. The arc being identical-to-3-decimals across a different-md5 render is suspicious — sanity-check whether `_arc_score_session.py` gave a fresh read or the composition is genuinely that close (this is the 0.487-reconciliation pattern: don't trust a single instrument run). The VISUAL says placement unchanged regardless, but I want your number.

**Direction (masking dead) — your read?** Two forks:
- (A) cheap placement probes: prompt-order swap, or swap the PuLID node assignment (man→node100 / aria→node103, i.e. who's the "dominant" last-spliced identity), or seed scan. ~$0.03 each.
- (B) PIVOT to the QUALITY axis: production-tier (`pulid.json`) for the over-cook — the dominant photorealism blocker per program intent. Gating unknown = does the man BIND at production tier (only ever shown at MAX). Needs a new driver (Rule #22 again).
I lean **B** — both identities already bind distinctly, so placement is a cosmetic/directorial axis we can cheaply tune or accept, while the over-cook is the real blocker to "photorealistic cinematic." Surfacing the same fork to the user. Pod RUNNING+BILLING. HEAD 48ad08b.

Cursor at send: 2026-06-13T05:50:55Z
